turns: We are drafting a Class II medical device submission.
The product is a wearable ECG patch for arrhythmia monitoring.
Regulatory path is FDA 510(k), predicate device K123456.
Primary endpoint: sensitivity >= 92% on AFib detection.
Secondary endpoint: false alarm rate below 0.15 per hour.
Pilot cohort size is 120 patients across 3 sites.
Risk control requires skin irritation biocompatibility test ISO 10993.
Data retention policy: keep raw ECG for 24 months.
Encryption at rest must be AES-256.
Timeline: verification complete by Q3, submission in Q4.
Decision: no cloud PHI export before legal sign-off.
Open issue: finalize adverse event adjudication workflow.

queries.q: What regulatory path did we choose?
What is our primary endpoint target?
Any hard constraint on PHI export?

turns: We are redesigning payout risk controls for cross-border merchants.
Settlement currency priority is USD then EUR.
Chargeback early-warning threshold is 1.2% weekly.
Reserve policy: high-risk merchants keep rolling reserve 8% for 90 days.
Fraud model refresh cadence is every 14 days.
Manual review SLA must stay under 6 hours.
Critical rule: block payouts if sanctions screen is unresolved.
Vendor choice: keep current KYC vendor until Q2 migration.
Decision: no instant payout for merchants with tenure < 60 days.
Incident channel is #risk-war-room with pager escalation.
Monthly audit sample size is 300 accounts.
Open issue: define exception approval matrix for strategic accounts.

queries.q: What threshold did we set for chargeback warning?
Any rule about instant payout eligibility?

turns: We are operating an internal LLM gateway for enterprise teams.
Default model route is gpt-5.3-codex for coding tasks.
Fallback model route is gpt-4.1-mini when latency spikes.
P95 latency target is under 4.5 seconds for chat endpoints.
Hard timeout is 20 seconds with graceful degrade response.
Context compaction is enabled for long threads.
Memory flush runs before auto-compaction when possible.
Retention for debug traces is 14 days in base tier.
PII in traces must be masked before persistence.
Decision: production changes require canary at 10% traffic first.
Rollback trigger: error rate increase above 2.5%.
Open issue: improve continuity after compaction for follow-up questions.

queries.q: What is our hard timeout policy?
What rollback trigger did we define?
How long do we retain debug traces in base tier?
